title.purpose: Buttons, provider names, selected states, and compact values.

body.purpose: Data cells, help text, descriptions, warnings, and control copy.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, table headers, rail labels, and compact metadata.

shadows.name: route-brief-ambient-lift
shadows.purpose: Reserved for the current route summary in the rail and rare anchored overlays.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard-visible focus on controls and fields.

motion.name: state-only
motion.purpose: Use for control feedback only. The current app intentionally avoids page choreography.

motion.name: spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinner only, disabled by reduced-motion handling.

components.name: Primary Run Button
components.description: Forest-filled primary action for running research.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 48px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; border: 1px solid var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); color: var(--surface, oklch(0.988 0.011 86)); padding: 0 0.75rem; font: 760 1rem/1.35 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-btn-primary svg { width: 18px; height: 18px; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.04); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.34 0.075 156 / 0.18); }

components.name: Secondary Export Button
components.description: Compact paper button for CSV and PDF exports.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 40px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; border: 1px solid var(--line-strong, oklch(0.71 0.035 82)); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface, oklch(0.988 0.011 86)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.205 0.025 155)); padding: 0 0.75rem; font: 760 0.88rem/1.35 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-btn-secondary svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.34 0.075 156 / 0.18); }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Warm paper text field for custom enrichment fields and brief controls.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.435 0.028 150)); font: 760 0.82rem/1.2 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-field input { height: 42px; border: 1px solid var(--line-strong, oklch(0.71 0.035 82)); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-input, oklch(0.995 0.008 86)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.205 0.025 155)); padding: 0 0.75rem; font: 400 0.88rem/1.5 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-field input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.34 0.075 156 / 0.18); outline: none; }

components.name: Selected Field Chip
components.css: .ds-chip-selected { min-height: 34px; max-width: 100%; border: 1px solid var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); border-radius: 999px; background: var(--forest-soft, oklch(0.91 0.047 150)); color: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); padding: 0 0.75rem; font: 760 0.88rem/1.35 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; overflow-wrap: anywhere; } .ds-chip-selected:hover { filter: brightness(0.99); } .ds-chip-selected:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.34 0.075 156 / 0.18); }

components.name: Provider Tile
components.description: Selectable provider option with cost, speed, and confidence metadata.
components.css: .ds-provider-tile { width: 100%; display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 1rem; border: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.835 0.029 86)); border-radius: 8px; background: oklch(0.98 0.012 86); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.205 0.025 155)); text-align: start; } .ds-provider-tile:hover { border-color: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); background: var(--forest-soft, oklch(0.91 0.047 150)); } .ds-provider-tile:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.34 0.075 156 / 0.18); } .ds-provider-title, .ds-provider-metrics { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 0.75rem; } .ds-provider-title strong { font: 760 1rem/1.35 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-provider-title em { border-radius: 999px; background: var(--surface-muted, oklch(0.935 0.028 88)); color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.435 0.028 150)); padding: 0.25rem 0.45rem; font: 780 0.72rem/1.2 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; font-style: normal; } .ds-provider-tile small { color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.435 0.028 150)); line-height: 1.35; } .ds-provider-metrics { color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.435 0.028 150)); font: 400 0.74rem/1.35 SFMono-Regular, SF Mono, Consolas, monospace; }

components.name: Metric Tile
components.description: Compact run metric for rows, cost, mode, and latency.
components.css: .ds-metric { min-width: 0; display: grid; grid-template-columns: 18px minmax(0, 1fr); gap: 0.18rem 0.5rem; padding: 0.75rem; border: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.835 0.029 86)); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-muted, oklch(0.935 0.028 88)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.205 0.025 155)); } .ds-metric svg { grid-row: span 2; width: 15px; height: 15px; color: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); } .ds-metric small { color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.435 0.028 150)); font: 720 0.74rem/1.2 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-metric strong { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; font: 400 0.88rem/1.35 SFMono-Regular, SF Mono, Consolas, monospace; }

components.name: Results Table
components.description: Dense cited output table with sticky uppercase headers and ruled rows.
components.css: .ds-table-wrap { overflow: auto; border: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.835 0.029 86)); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-input, oklch(0.995 0.008 86)); } .ds-table { width: 100%; min-width: 680px; border-collapse: collapse; } .ds-table th, .ds-table td { min-width: 128px; max-width: 320px; padding: 0.85rem; border-block-end: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.835 0.029 86)); text-align: start; vertical-align: top; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-table th { position: sticky; top: 0; background: var(--table-header, oklch(0.93 0.027 86)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.205 0.025 155)); font: 780 0.74rem/1.2 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-table td { color: oklch(0.28 0.026 155); font: 400 0.88rem/1.4 Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif; overflow-wrap: anywhere; } .ds-table a { color: var(--blue, oklch(0.43 0.086 245)); font-weight: 720; text-decoration: none; } .ds-table a:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-confidence { display: inline-flex; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--forest-soft, oklch(0.91 0.047 150)); color: var(--forest, oklch(0.34 0.075 156)); padding: 0.24rem 0.5rem; font: 760 0.76rem/1.2 SFMono-Regular, SF Mono, Consolas, monospace; }

narrative.overview:
CT Search should feel like a precise capital-markets worksheet with the judgment of a trusted research analyst. The design is light, editorial, dense, and restrained: warm paper surfaces, exact borders, serif headings used sparingly, compact controls, and enough color to show status without turning the workspace into decoration.

The reference triangle is deliberate: Linear for density, typography, crisp figures, and control ergonomics; Lightfield for minimal product language and systems-minded primitives; Rogo for private-capital language, institutional trust, and finance-specific positioning.

The system rejects generic AI-chat aesthetics, oversized SaaS heroes, neon gradients, glassmorphism, decorative card grids, noisy chrome, and hidden provider logic. The first screen is the working product surface: upload, route, review, export.
keyCharacteristics: Dense, table-first workspace with stable columns and persistent export affordances.
Warm neutral surfaces with forest as the operator accent, brass and rust reserved for metadata and risk.
Crisp 8px controls, thin borders, compact typography, and visible focus states.
Progressive disclosure for provider routing, confidence, cost, warnings, and citations.

rules.name: The Evidence Color Rule
rules.body: Blue belongs to sources and navigable citations. Forest belongs to operation and selection. Do not swap them.

rules.name: The No Pure Neutral Rule
rules.body: Never introduce `#000` or `#fff`; all neutrals stay gently warm or green-tinted.

rules.name: The Serif Restraint Rule
rules.body: Use Georgia for brand, panel, and empty-state headings only. Never use display serif in buttons, labels, metrics, or data tables.

rules.name: The Figure Clarity Rule
rules.body: Costs, latency, provider scores, and compact estimates use the mono stack so figures line up and read as audit data.

rules.name: The Ruled Surface Rule
rules.body: Use borders and tonal changes before shadows. If a shadow appears on a repeated table, metric tile, or provider list item, it is probably too much.

dos: Do keep the first viewport as the actual workbench: upload, route, review, export.
Do use forest for selected controls, run actions, focus rings, confidence chips, and live states.
Do preserve compact 8px controls, precise borders, and table-first density.
Do expose provider, cost, confidence, citations, warnings, and exports near the workflow they affect.
Do keep reduced-motion support and visible keyboard focus on every interactive element.
donts: Don't use generic AI-chat aesthetics, oversized SaaS heroes, neon gradients, glassmorphism, decorative card grids, or noisy chrome.
Don't make the first viewport a marketing landing page.
Don't hide provider choice, confidence, citations, warnings, or export affordances behind theatrical flows.
Don't use `border-left` or `border-right` greater than 1px as colored accents on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts.
Don't introduce pure black, pure white, gradient text, decorative motion, or full-saturation inactive states.
